category_title: Independent students who struggle with spatial visualization in three-dimensional geometry problems
scenario_title: 3D Pythagorean Theorem truck problem with 2D diagram
minimal_context: Lisa is working independently at her desk to solve a word problem about fitting a long pole in a loading truck using a diagram of a rectangular prism.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Here is the multistep word problem about fitting a long pole in the back of a loading truck. You can use this diagram of a rectangular prism with the labeled dimensions to solve it.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks at the diagram and her scratch paper) Is that supposed to be the way it is farthest in the truck?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think based on the picture?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Is the next one going to be in here? Is it still Pythagorean Theorem?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just try your best to calculate the answer using the numbers provided in the diagram.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Starts to guess again using a different combination of dimensions.) I’m not sure yet, because we don’t know the whole truck.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Here is the multistep word problem about fitting a long pole in the back of a loading truck. You can use this diagram of a rectangular prism with the labeled dimensions to solve it.

STUDENT: (Looks at the diagram and her scratch paper) Is that supposed to be the way it is farthest in the truck?

TUTOR: What do you think based on the picture?

STUDENT: Is the next one going to be in here? Is it still Pythagorean Theorem?

TUTOR: Just try your best to calculate the answer using the numbers provided in the diagram.

STUDENT: (Starts to guess again using a different combination of dimensions.) I’m not sure yet, because we don’t know the whole truck.

task.content: fitting a long pole in the back of a loading truck, but the provided diagram consisted only of a rectangular prism with some labeled dimensions
task.learning_goal: solve a multistep, word problem that required three-dimensional use of the Pythagorean Theorem

context.materials: diagram consisted only of a rectangular prism with some labeled dimensions

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: multistep, word problem that required three-dimensional use of the Pythagorean Theorem
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Starts to guess again using a different combination of dimensions.
related_quotes: I’m not sure yet, because we don’t know the whole truck.

behavior_patterns.condition: Even with the semiconcrete visual and verbal supports
related_quotes: Is that supposed to be the way it is farthest in the truck?
Is the next one going to be in here?
